assertions.expectation: Explains the key difference: surge upgrades nodes in-place with rolling replacement, blue-green creates an entirely new pool
assertions.reason: Response clearly explains that blue-green creates a new pool ('GKE provisions 9 new nodes (green pool)') and contrasts this with surge approach that drains nodes immediately

assertions.expectation: Recommends blue-green for this scenario due to local SSDs (data can't migrate between nodes)
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends blue-green upgrade and clearly states the reason: 'Surge drains nodes immediately, destroying local SSD data' and 'Local SSD data preserved until you explicitly confirm the new ring is healthy'

assertions.expectation: Explains that local SSD data is lost when a node is drained during surge upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Surge drains nodes immediately, destroying local SSD data' and identifies this as a key problem with surge upgrades for this use case

assertions.expectation: Recommends GKE's autoscaled blue-green upgrade strategy configured with --enable-autoscaling and --autoscaled-rollout-policy (NOT surge parameters like maxSurge/maxUnavailable which don't apply to blue-green), or describes the blue-green process if manual workflow is needed
assertions.reason: Response uses incorrect parameters - it shows --standard-rollout-policy instead of --autoscaled-rollout-policy and doesn't include --enable-autoscaling. The parameters shown (batch-node-count, batch-soak-duration) are not the correct blue-green autoscaling configuration

assertions.expectation: Addresses Cassandra-specific concerns: need to decommission from the ring before draining, rebuild on new nodes
assertions.reason: Response thoroughly addresses Cassandra concerns including 'Cassandra nodes need proper decommissioning (nodetool decommission)' and provides detailed steps for decommissioning during the soak period

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for creating the new node pool with matching configuration
assertions.reason: Response provides gcloud commands for configuring the upgrade strategy but doesn't show commands for creating a new node pool with matching configuration (node type, local SSDs, etc.)

assertions.expectation: Includes kubectl commands for cordoning and draining
assertions.reason: Response mentions that nodes are cordoned and drained as part of the blue-green process but doesn't provide the actual kubectl commands for cordoning and draining

assertions.expectation: Mentions PDB configuration to protect the Cassandra quorum during migration
assertions.reason: Response includes a complete PodDisruptionBudget configuration with 'minAvailable: 7 # Maintain majority quorum (7 out of 9)' to protect Cassandra quorum
